title: aster tripolium subsp. longicaulis
intro: Aster tripolium subsp. longicaulis, also known as Sea Aster or Sea Starwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that grows in coastal areas and is characterized by its yellow flowers and long stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Aster tripolium subsp. longicaulis include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Aster tripolium subsp. longicaulis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent, and can be used to treat a variety of ailments.
